design_philosophy.core_loop: Select historical fighter, compete in tournament bracket format, defeat opponents through 2-of-3 round victories, progress to boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-on-one combat execution with risk/reward positioning and move timing against varied opponent movesets
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a historical warrior and prove dominance in a global fighter tournament; test skill against iconic figures from different eras
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Thematic commitment to historical/legendary fighters as roster; introduces 'Death Match' mode with environmental hazards and non-standard 'seesaw lifebar' mechanic that rewards sustained aggression differently than traditional fighting games

difficulty.checkpointing: Round-based progression; must win 2-of-3 rounds per opponent match
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Mode selection between Normal Game and Death Match provides difficulty variance; Death Match adds environmental hazards and altered win conditions

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament bracket structure: defeat 6 new characters, then 4 randomly-selected original characters, then 2 sequential bosses (Neo-Geegus, Dio)
replayability_hooks: Random opponent selection in mid-tournament creates varied match sequences
Death Match mode with environmental hazards and seesaw lifebar mechanic
14 distinct playable characters with unique movesets encourage multiple playthroughs
Two-boss final gauntlet requires different strategies

world_building_techniques: Character roster as world-building (historical/legendary fighters from different cultures and eras)
Tournament framing device (Dr. Brown's World Heroes tournament seeking 'strongest fighter of history')
Boss narrative presence (Geegus as tournament interference threat; Dio as final challenger)

boss_design.philosophy: Sequential boss encounters punctuate tournament progression; Neo-Geegus serves as semi-final threat (previous tournament interference), Dio as final boss representing ultimate opponent
notable_bosses: Neo-Geegus (semi-final boss; represents continued threat from first tournament)

faction_indicators: Regional/cultural fighter representation (Japanese: Kotaro Fuuma, Hanzou Hattori, Ryoko Izumo; European: Jeanne D'Arc, Rasputin; others: Captain Kidd, Muscle Power, Johnny Maximum)
danger_signals: Neo-Geegus as recurring antagonist representing chaos/interference in orderly competition
Death Match mode's hazardous stages and seesaw lifebar mechanic introduce environmental danger
environmental_storytelling: Fighter selection implicitly communicates historical period and cultural context
Death Match stages feature unique environmental hazards that create thematic combat arenas
